title: Missing Null Check in String Concatenation

statement.markdown: Given a string and a character, return the string appended with a second string formed by concatenating the given character repeated the same number of times as the given string's length. However, the function will return a wrong result if the given string is null.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a string and a character.
statement.outputFormat: Output will be the string appended with the repeated character.
statement.notes: Ensure null string check is included.

examples.explanation: Input string "ab" has 2 characters, so the repeated character is "cc"

hints.text: Consider handling the case where the input string is null.

editorial.title: Editorial - Handling Null Input String
editorial.intuition: A null input string requires special handling.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach does not handle null string properly
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution checks for null string before processing input.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution handles all inputs, including null.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run handles null string properly.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string myFunction(string s, char c) { return s+c; } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public String myFunction(String s, char c) { return s+c; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def my_function(self, s, c): pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string myFunction(string s, char c) { return s+c; } }
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public String myFunction(String s, char c) { return s+c; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def my_function(self, s, c): return s+c
